paper_title: Experimental Study of Bone Marrow Preservation and Processing Marks

methods.method_name: Experimental Study of Bone Marrow Preservation and Processing Marks
methods.description: Experimental study using red deer metapodials exposed to different environmental conditions and durations to evaluate marrow preservation, determine nutritional value decay over time (chemical analysis mentioned but not detailed here), and identify taphonomic signatures of secondary processing (skinning and bone breakage for marrow extraction).

steps.description: Obtain adult or prime-adult red deer (C. elaphus) metapodials from the Boumourt National Game Reserve (Pallars Jussà, Lleida, Spain), systematically separated from the forelimbs and hindlimbs at the carpals and tarsals. Record the cut marks inflicted by rangers using modern steel knives when separating the metapodials from the rest of the carcass.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain and prepare red deer metapodials for experimental exposure, recording initial marks.

steps.description: Divide 79 metapodials (38 metacarpals and 41 metatarsals) into three experimental series corresponding to three different environmental scenarios: Scenario 1 (outdoor autumn, 1-9 weeks exposure), Scenario 2 (outdoor spring, 1-9 weeks exposure), and Scenario 3 (indoor simulation of Israel's Mediterranean climate, 1-4 weeks exposure). Scenario 3 also included a 'skinless' variant.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up three environmental exposure scenarios for red deer metapodials with varying durations and conditions.
inputs: Environmental conditions (outdoor autumn, outdoor spring, indoor climate simulation)

steps.description: Each week, up to five metapodials were removed from the subaerial exposure: two to perform chemical analyses on the nutritional values of the marrow and two/three for processing (skinning and breaking the bone open for the marrow). This was performed systematically in the first two series in outdoor conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample exposed metapodials weekly for subsequent analysis and processing.
outputs: Weekly samples of exposed metapodials (subsets for chemical analysis and processing)

steps.description: Process sampled metapodials (two/three per week from outdoor series) by skinning and breaking the bone open for the marrow. Skin/hide extraction was performed with flint flakes, and the marrow was accessed using hammerstone percussion with quartzite percussion tools. The secondary processing was always performed by the same individual, with no guidelines on how to extract the marrow. Observe and record the types, frequencies, and characteristics of marks (incisions, sawing marks, chops, chipped marks) resulting from this processing.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform secondary processing (skinning, bone breakage) on sampled bones using specified tools and analyze the resulting taphonomic marks.
outputs: Data on types, frequencies, and characteristics of taphonomic marks

steps.description: Perform chemical analyses on the nutritional values of the marrow from a subset of weekly samples (two per week from outdoor series). (Note: Specific chemical analysis methods are not detailed in the provided text sections).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform chemical analysis on bone marrow for nutritional value.
inputs: Bone marrow samples (from sampled metapodials, subset for chemical analysis)
outputs: Data on marrow nutritional value over time
